steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: The greatest common factor of the coefficients $186$ and $30$ is $6$. Both terms also include $t^2$, so factor out $6t^2$.

steps.title: Factor each term
steps.content:
Divide each term by $6t^2$:
$$\frac{186t^3}{6t^2}=31t$$
$$\frac{30t^2u}{6t^2}=5u$$
The expression factors as $6t^2(31t-5u)$. Check the choices:
  • Choice A - $6t^{2}(31t^{2}-5u)$: the first term would have one extra factor of $t$.
  • Choice B - $6t^{2}(31t-5u)$: multiplying gives the original expression.
  • Choice C - $6tu(31t^{2}-5)$: introduces an extra $u$ in the first term.
  • Choice D - $6t^{2}u(31t-5)$: also adds an extra $u$ to the first term.
The answer is B.
